title: Reolink Argus 3 Ultra 4K WiFi Cam with Solar Panel & Colour Night Vision $139.99 Delivered (RRP $199.99) @ Reolink AU
body:
Deal: Reolink Argus 3 Ultra 4K WiFi Cam with Solar Panel & Colour Night Vision $139.99 Delivered (RRP $199.99) @ Reolink AU

[dealbest] Use code imreo5auz on the Reolink website and it drops to AU$132.99 delivered.
[NBSales] OP any EoFY deals on PoE cameras that are closer to this discount?
[dealbest] I will post a deal for the Duo 3V PoE tomorrow, should be around AU$199.99 delivered.
[busterau] Dont install these outside, i've got 2 of these outside for just over 12 months and both have stopped working. Water behind the lens and when I took them down and shook they were filled with water. Bought them refurbished from Reolink so only had 12 months warranty.
[apok00] Had this issue with the argus 3 pro but not the argus 3 ultra version. Cameras in the same spot, not under an eve. I know they both have the same waterproof rating so maybe just a fault on the pro i had.
I could shake it around and hear water.
[homerc] I have an Argus 3 Pro with a solar panel from a previous deal. While overall I think it's a great entry level surveillance camera, I find the object detection quite poor. It either gives lots of false detections OR it doesn't turn the camera on quick enough when someone shows up. Last week some random guy walked up to our carport in the middle of the night on a bike, tried to open the car doors (thankfully they were all locked), then rode away again. The delay between detection and recording means the camera only started recording when he was already on his way out, so the footage was unusable.
I am not sure how much better the Ultra is to the Pro, but I suspect not much. I have read that the detection mechanism doesn't work as well if the person comes directly towards the camera (in my case at a 45 degree angle), so if you have a better spot to install your camera, the detection may work better.
[Weshouldgetsushi] I've the same problem with solar/battery cameras. Gave up.
[miku] Unfortunate limitation of solar/battery cameras relying on PIR before waking the actual camera/object detection logic. If you want static wide coverage from a single camera I'd invest in PoE, but otherwise maybe play with the camera location/angles to take into account the weaknesses of PIR.
[KpopSemenHunter] Yep. Same experience here. Object detection technology on wifi cameras is still poor and will often miss events.
[UncleRico] They’re decent as far as battery cams go, but battery cams should always be regarded as a last resort.
I’ve got a couple of these and a couple of the Argus 3 Pros (plus 30 other Reolinks). These have a better 8mp resolution vs the 5mp of the 3 Pro, but the biggest advantage was always the inclusion of the 6w Solar Panel 2 instead of the 3w Solar Panel of this pack. Disappointing, since its omission deprives us of one of the main reasons to upgrade, especially for anyone who doesn’t get enough sun to keep these running 24/7/365. The other difference is the marginally improved field of view, but it’s not huge.
Wherever possible, POE is profoundly better, not just for object detection, especially for objects moving towards the camera (which all Argus struggle with) instead of across the field of view, but even moreso for the game-changing capabilities of preroll rather than waiting for the camera to awaken after IR detection.
[kevindo] I have 3 bought from AliExpress, under $100 each. It's been working fine after 1 and half years. One of them the metal part got rusty. The motion detection was not so good. Sometimes giving false alarm and it only recorded for 8 seconds once triggered. U need to purchase their cloud save( where they make more money) to view longer record. Would not recommend overall. Might want to try tapo when this one is down.
